intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can reason about Express production performance across code, runtime, and infrastructure layers.
primaryQuestion: An Express API has poor p95 latency under load. How would you investigate and improve performance without guessing?

expectedSignals.label: Starts with measurement: p50/p95/p99 latency, throughput, error rate, CPU, memory, event loop delay, database timing, and downstream timing.

requirements.description: Looks at p50, p95, and p99 latency rather than only averages.
Compares CPU, memory, event loop delay, database timing, and downstream timing.
Breaks the problem down by endpoint, status code, and throughput under load.

expectedSignals.label: Looks for synchronous work, blocking I/O, excessive JSON parsing/stringifying, inefficient logging, and slow middleware.

requirements.description: Uses event loop lag or profiling to distinguish blocking JS work from slow I/O.
Examines synchronous filesystem, crypto, JSON, serialization, and logging costs in request handlers.
Measures middleware and route timing instead of assuming the database is always the bottleneck.

expectedSignals.label: Considers compression, caching, reverse proxy, load balancing, clustering, and NODE_ENV=production.

requirements.description: Explains when compression saves bandwidth and when CPU cost can worsen latency.
Considers cache headers, reverse proxy, or CDN for suitable responses and static assets.
Mentions clustering/process management and NODE_ENV=production for production behavior.

expectedSignals.label: Validates changes with load tests and production telemetry rather than micro-optimizing blindly.

requirements.description: Compares metrics before and after a change under representative load.
Ensures latency gains do not increase errors, CPU saturation, or downstream pressure.
Confirms improvements with production dashboards, traces, or canary data.

scoringHints.description: Presents a measurement-led plan across Express middleware, Node runtime, database/downstream dependencies, caching, compression, process model, and verification.
Covers common Express performance practices but misses one layer such as downstream tracing or validation.
Lists optimizations without a diagnostic method.
Answer does not show production performance engineering skill.

followUps.question: Which metric would tell you the event loop is blocked rather than the database being slow?
When can gzip compression make latency worse?
followUps.purpose: Check CPU versus bandwidth trade-off reasoning.

en.title: Express performance investigation
en.displayQuestion: An Express API has poor p95 latency under load. How would you investigate and improve performance without guessing?
en.displayIntent: Check production performance diagnosis and improvement strategy.
guidance: Start from metrics and traces.
Cover code, middleware, runtime, database, cache, and infrastructure layers.
commonMistakes: Scaling before identifying the bottleneck.
Ignoring synchronous request-path work.

vi.title: Điều tra performance Express
vi.displayQuestion: Một Express API có p95 latency kém khi chịu tải. Bạn sẽ điều tra và cải thiện như thế nào mà không đoán mò?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra chiến lược chẩn đoán và cải thiện performance production.
guidance: Bắt đầu từ metric và trace.
Bao phủ code, middleware, runtime, database, cache và infrastructure.
commonMistakes: Scale trước khi biết bottleneck.
Bỏ qua synchronous work trong request path.

ja.title: Express performance investigation
ja.displayQuestion: Express API が load 下で p95 latency が悪いです。推測ではなく、どう調査して改善しますか。
ja.displayIntent: production performance diagnosis と improvement strategy を確認する。
guidance: metrics と traces から始める。
code、middleware、runtime、database、cache、infrastructure を扱う。
commonMistakes: bottleneck 特定前に scale する。
request path の synchronous work を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: Express performance best practices
sourceReferences.note: Used for compression, avoiding synchronous functions, logging, NODE_ENV, clustering, caching, load balancers, and reverse proxy guidance.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can design health checks and graceful shutdown for Express services in production deployments.
primaryQuestion: How would you implement health checks and graceful shutdown for an Express service running in containers or behind a load balancer?

expectedSignals.label: Separates liveness from readiness and includes dependency health where appropriate.

requirements.description: Uses liveness to indicate the process should remain running.
Uses readiness to indicate whether the service should receive traffic.
Includes database, cache, or queue health in readiness when those dependencies are required to serve requests.

expectedSignals.label: On shutdown signal, stops accepting new connections and allows in-flight requests to finish within a deadline.

requirements.description: Registers process signal handling for shutdown initiated by containers or orchestration.
Uses server.close or equivalent to stop accepting fresh requests.
Allows active requests to complete but enforces a maximum drain deadline.

expectedSignals.label: Closes database, message queue, and other resource pools cleanly.

requirements.description: Mentions closing database pools after traffic drain begins.
Includes message queues, cache clients, or other long-lived external connections.
Logs or monitors resource close failures and forced shutdowns.

expectedSignals.label: Coordinates with load balancer or orchestrator readiness so traffic drains before process exit.

requirements.description: Changes readiness to unhealthy or not-ready before exiting so new traffic stops.
Accounts for load balancer or orchestrator delay before terminating the process.
Prevents new requests after resources have begun closing.

scoringHints.description: Covers readiness/liveness semantics, signal handling, server.close, inflight deadlines, dependency cleanup, traffic draining, and observability.
Understands graceful shutdown basics but misses one orchestration or dependency detail.
Can add /health but lacks lifecycle management.
Answer is not sufficient for production deployment safety.

followUps.question: What should readiness return while the app is draining during shutdown?
What would you monitor to know shutdown is causing dropped requests?

en.title: Health checks and graceful shutdown
en.displayQuestion: How would you implement health checks and graceful shutdown for an Express service running in containers or behind a load balancer?
en.displayIntent: Check production lifecycle management for Express services.
guidance: Separate liveness, readiness, and dependency health.
Discuss SIGTERM, server.close, request draining, deadlines, and resource cleanup.
commonMistakes: Exiting immediately on SIGTERM.
Returning healthy while critical dependencies are down.

vi.title: Health check và graceful shutdown
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn sẽ implement health check và graceful shutdown cho Express service chạy trong container hoặc sau load balancer như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra quản lý lifecycle production cho Express service.
guidance: Tách liveness, readiness và dependency health.
Nói về SIGTERM, server.close, drain request, deadline và cleanup resource.
commonMistakes: Thoát process ngay khi nhận SIGTERM.
Trả healthy khi dependency critical đang down.

ja.title: health check と graceful shutdown
ja.displayQuestion: container または load balancer 配下の Express service で health checks と graceful shutdown をどう実装しますか。
ja.displayIntent: Express service の production lifecycle management を確認する。
guidance: liveness、readiness、dependency health を分ける。
SIGTERM、server.close、request draining、deadline、resource cleanup を説明する。
commonMistakes: SIGTERM で即 exit する。
critical dependency が down でも healthy を返す。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Health checks and graceful shutdown
sourceReferences.note: Used for health check and graceful shutdown production patterns.

sourceReferences.label: Express performance best practices
sourceReferences.note: Used for reliability and automatic restart context.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can structure a larger Express service with clear boundaries between routing, controllers, services, repositories, policies, and cross-cutting middleware.
primaryQuestion: For a growing Express codebase, how would you structure routes, controllers, services, repositories, middleware, and configuration so the app stays maintainable?

expectedSignals.label: Keeps Express-specific concerns near routing/controllers and domain logic in services or domain modules.

requirements.description: Keeps req, res, and route concerns in routers/controllers while domain decisions live in services or modules.
Explains when simple route-local logic is acceptable versus when service extraction pays off.
Avoids large route handlers that combine validation, persistence, policy, and response shaping.

expectedSignals.label: Uses explicit dependency boundaries for databases, external clients, configuration, and policies.

requirements.description: Places persistence logic behind repositories, data access modules, or explicit service dependencies.
Keeps environment parsing and validated config out of scattered route code.
Uses explicit dependencies for external APIs and policies so tests and ownership stay manageable.

expectedSignals.label: Avoids turning middleware into a dumping ground for business logic.

requirements.description: Uses middleware for concerns like auth, validation, logging, and request context.
Keeps business workflows and invariants in domain/service modules.
Connects overloaded middleware to poor reuse, hidden control flow, or hard-to-test behavior.

expectedSignals.label: Designs for testability, consistent errors, observability, and gradual feature ownership.

requirements.description: Describes boundaries that allow HTTP-level and service-level testing with controlled dependencies.
Uses shared error handling, logging, metrics, or tracing patterns across features.
Organizes modules so teams can own features without editing one monolithic app file.

scoringHints.description: Balances pragmatic Express simplicity with clear module boundaries, dependency management, testability, observability, and team ownership.
Has a reasonable layered structure but misses one cross-cutting concern or dependency boundary.
Suggests folders but cannot explain responsibilities and trade-offs.
Answer does not show maintainable backend architecture thinking.

followUps.question: When would you keep logic in a route handler instead of extracting a service?
How would your structure make auth, validation, and error handling consistent across teams?

en.title: Maintainable Express architecture
en.displayQuestion: For a growing Express codebase, how would you structure routes, controllers, services, repositories, middleware, and configuration so the app stays maintainable?
en.displayIntent: Check architecture boundaries and maintainability judgment.
guidance: Discuss responsibilities, dependencies, testing, errors, and observability.
Avoid framework-heavy answers unless justified by the problem.
commonMistakes: Putting business logic in route handlers.
Adding abstractions without a reason.

vi.title: Kiến trúc Express dễ maintain
vi.displayQuestion: Với một Express codebase đang lớn dần, bạn sẽ tổ chức routes, controllers, services, repositories, middleware và configuration thế nào để app dễ maintain?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ranh giới kiến trúc và judgment về maintainability.
guidance: Nói về trách nhiệm, dependency, testing, error và observability.
Tránh câu trả lời quá nặng framework nếu không có lý do.
commonMistakes: Đưa business logic vào route handler.
Thêm abstraction mà không có vấn đề thật.

ja.title: maintainable な Express architecture
ja.displayQuestion: 成長する Express codebase で、routes、controllers、services、repositories、middleware、configuration をどう構成して maintainable に保ちますか。
ja.displayIntent: architecture boundary と maintainability judgment を確認する。
guidance: responsibilities、dependencies、testing、errors、observability を説明する。
問題がないのに heavy framework 的な回答にしない。
commonMistakes: business logic を route handler に置く。
理由なく abstraction を増やす。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Using middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for middleware boundaries and app/router-level composition.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Database integration
sourceReferences.note: Used for keeping database access as an integration concern rather than inside route definitions.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can plan an Express 4 to Express 5 migration with attention to route matching, removed APIs, async error behavior, body parsing defaults, and regression testing.
primaryQuestion: What would you check when migrating an Express 4 service to Express 5, and how would you reduce the risk of breaking routes or error handling?

expectedSignals.label: Runs automated tests and reviews route matching syntax changes such as named wildcards and optional segments.

requirements.description: Runs or adds regression tests for critical route patterns before changing Express versions.
Mentions Express 5 path syntax changes such as named wildcards replacing bare /* patterns.
Reviews optional segment and route matching differences that can alter URL behavior.

expectedSignals.label: Removes deprecated response signatures like res.send(body, status) and res.json(obj, status).

requirements.description: Searches for Express 4 response overloads removed or changed in Express 5.
Uses res.status(code).send(body) or res.status(code).json(body) instead.
Covers validation, error, redirect, and not-found responses after rewriting.

expectedSignals.label: Understands rejected promises from async handlers are forwarded in Express 5.

requirements.description: States that rejected promises from async handlers are forwarded to error handling in Express 5.
Checks whether existing async wrappers or try/catch patterns still behave correctly.
Tests rejected promise paths and centralized error responses after migration.

expectedSignals.label: Checks express.urlencoded defaults, req.body behavior, static dotfile changes, and app.listen error callback behavior.

requirements.description: Verifies express.urlencoded defaults and req.body behavior for endpoints relying on parsed forms.
Reviews static file dotfile serving changes that could break well-known or hidden asset paths.
Monitors route 404s, 5xx rate, latency, auth/login failures, and startup errors during canary rollout.

scoringHints.description: Covers breaking API removals, path syntax, async errors, parser/static defaults, codemods, compatibility tests, canary rollout, and rollback.
Understands main migration risks but misses one specific change or rollout detail.
Knows there is a new version but cannot identify concrete breaking changes.
Answer is not sufficient to trust a production migration.

followUps.question: How would a catch-all route written as /* need to change in Express 5?
What production signals would you watch after a canary upgrade?

en.title: Express 4 to Express 5 migration
en.displayQuestion: What would you check when migrating an Express 4 service to Express 5, and how would you reduce the risk of breaking routes or error handling?
en.displayIntent: Check migration planning and version-specific Express knowledge.
guidance: Mention route syntax, removed APIs, async errors, parser/static changes, and tests.
Discuss rollout and rollback controls.
commonMistakes: Assuming a drop-in upgrade.
Testing only the happy path.

vi.title: Migration Express 4 lên Express 5
vi.displayQuestion: Khi migrate một service từ Express 4 lên Express 5, bạn sẽ kiểm tra gì và giảm rủi ro vỡ route hoặc error handling thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra kế hoạch migration và kiến thức Express theo version.
guidance: Nhắc tới route syntax, API bị remove, async error, parser/static change và test.
Nói về rollout và rollback control.
commonMistakes: Cho rằng upgrade là drop-in.
Chỉ test happy path.

ja.title: Express 4 から Express 5 への migration
ja.displayQuestion: Express 4 service を Express 5 に migrate するとき何を確認し、route や error handling の破壊リスクをどう下げますか。
ja.displayIntent: migration planning と version-specific Express knowledge を確認する。
guidance: route syntax、removed APIs、async errors、parser/static changes、tests に触れる。
rollout と rollback control を説明する。
commonMistakes: drop-in upgrade と考える。
happy path だけ test する。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Moving to Express 5
sourceReferences.note: Used for Express 5 breaking changes, async promise handling, path syntax, parser defaults, and static dotfile changes.

intent: Evaluate senior-level incident response for Express APIs, including triage, rollback, observability, customer impact, and durable fixes.
primaryQuestion: A new Express release causes a spike in 500s and login latency. How would you triage, mitigate, and follow up?

expectedSignals.label: Checks error rate, latency percentiles, affected routes, deploy timing, logs, traces, and dependency health.

requirements.description: Starts with 5xx rate, latency percentiles, traffic volume, saturation, and affected endpoints.
Compares the spike to release timing, config changes, and feature flags.
Checks route-level logs, traces, database/session store health, and downstream errors.

expectedSignals.label: Mitigates with rollback, feature flag disablement, traffic shift, or rate limiting depending on blast radius.

requirements.description: Prioritizes reducing user impact before extended root-cause debugging.
Chooses rollback, feature flag disablement, traffic shift, or rate limiting based on affected scope.
Confirms that 500s and login latency improve after mitigation.

expectedSignals.label: Looks for common Express-specific causes such as auth middleware order, body parser limits, session store issues, trust proxy changes, or async error handling regressions.

requirements.description: Investigates middleware ordering or auth changes that could slow or fail login routes.
Reviews body parser limits, session store behavior, cookie settings, and trust proxy changes.
Looks for Express async error handling changes that could convert failures into 500s or hangs.

expectedSignals.label: Communicates impact, owns a post-incident review, adds tests/alerts, and validates the durable fix.

requirements.description: Shares scope, customer impact, mitigation status, and next update cadence with stakeholders.
Adds tests, monitors, or alerts for the failure mode that caused the incident.
Confirms the permanent fix through canary, load checks, or production telemetry before closing follow-up.

scoringHints.description: Balances rapid mitigation with evidence-based diagnosis, Express-specific hypotheses, stakeholder communication, and durable prevention.
Has a reasonable incident flow but misses one dimension such as communication or permanent fix validation.
Can debug technically but does not manage customer impact or follow-up quality.
Answer does not show senior production ownership.

followUps.question: Which dashboard panels would you open in the first five minutes?
What Express-specific regressions would you check first for login latency?

en.title: Express production incident response
en.displayQuestion: A new Express release causes a spike in 500s and login latency. How would you triage, mitigate, and follow up?
en.displayIntent: Check senior incident response and Express-specific production reasoning.
guidance: Start with impact, telemetry, and deploy correlation.
Discuss mitigation, root cause, communication, and durable prevention.
commonMistakes: Debugging too long before mitigation.
Rolling back without a follow-up fix or test.

vi.title: Ứng phó incident Express production
vi.displayQuestion: Một release Express mới làm tăng 500s và latency login. Bạn sẽ triage, mitigate và follow up như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra incident response senior và reasoning production theo Express.
guidance: Bắt đầu từ impact, telemetry và tương quan với deploy.
Nói về mitigation, root cause, communication và phòng ngừa lâu dài.
commonMistakes: Debug quá lâu trước khi mitigate.
Rollback xong nhưng không có fix hoặc test follow-up.

ja.title: Express production incident response
ja.displayQuestion: 新しい Express release により 500s と login latency が急増しました。どう triage、mitigate、follow up しますか。
ja.displayIntent: senior incident response と Express-specific production reasoning を確認する。
guidance: impact、telemetry、deploy correlation から始める。
mitigation、root cause、communication、durable prevention を説明する。
commonMistakes: mitigation 前に長く debug しすぎる。
rollback 後に follow-up fix や test を追加しない。

sourceReferences.label: Express performance best practices
sourceReferences.note: Used for production reliability, logging, exception handling, restarts, load balancers, and reverse proxy context.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
